item.title: Undergraduate law symposium spring 2026 held at NSU
item.description: The Centre for Legal Research in collaboration with department of law at North South University organised the NSU Undergraduate Law Symposium Spring 2026 in Dhaka on Saturday....

item.title: Prime accused in Nawapara businessman murder case held
item.description: The police on Sunday night arrested Shah Mahmud, 38, from Jashore town, who is the prime accused in the case for the killing of Anisur Rahman, executive director of Joint Trading Corporation....

item.title: UAE envoy calls on PM’s defence adviser
item.description: The United Arab Emirates’ ambassador to Bangladesh Abdulla Ali Alhmoudi paid a courtesy call on prime minister’s defence adviser retired brigadier general AKM Shamsul Islam at his office at the Armed Forces Division in Dhaka Cantonment on Monday....Bangladeshi Culture Course

item.title: Passengers suffer as Rajshahi transport workers go on strike
item.description: Bus communication between Rajshahi and the rest of the country remained suspended on Monday as transport workers went on strike for an indefinite period without prior notice, causing immense suffering to passengers....

item.title: Police, students scuffle over graffiti in Chattogram
item.description: A scuffle broke out between the police and a group of students in Chattogram city on Monday as they tried to draw July uprising graffiti defying a ban on processions and rallies in the area....

item.title: Youth tortured to death over theft allegation
item.description: A 22-YEAR-OLD man was beaten to death at Alamdanga in Chuadanga on Sunday on allegation of van theft, with reports claiming he was subjected to severe torture, including hot water being poured into his nose....

item.title: US energy giants merge to meet AI demand
item.description: US energy giants NextEra Energy and Dominion Energy announced Monday they will combine to create the world’s largest regulated electric utility and help power surging demand from artificial intelligence data centers......

item.title: Oil prices dip on US sanctions relief for Iran
item.description: Oil prices turned lower in afternoon trading Monday and stock markets advanced after an Iranian media report said US officials had agreed to suspend sanctions against its crude while talks on ending the war continue......

item.title: Police want to show ex-CJ Khairul arrested in another July murder case
item.description: The police has petitioned the Dhaka Chief Metropolitan Magistrate Court to show jailed former chief justice ABM Khairul Haque arrested in a murder case filed with the Jatrabari police station over a killing during the 2024 July-August mass uprising....Geographic Reference

item.title: China’s Baidu posts quarterly revenue drop
item.description: Chinese tech giant Baidu posted a fourth straight drop in quarterly revenue on Monday, as the firm looks to offset losses in its traditional ad business with growth in artificial intelligence......

item.title: NCC Bank celebrates 33rd anniv
item.description: NCC Bank celebrated its 33rd founding anniversary on Sunday with a commitment to sustainable growth, customer trust, innovation, and the transition to Shariah-based banking......

item.title: Paramount Insurance declares 10 pc cash dividend for 2025
item.description: Paramount Insurance PLC recently organised its 27th annual general meeting using an online platform, where shareholders unanimously approved a 10 per cent cash dividend for the year 2025. Chairman of the company, Shakhawat Hossain, presided over the meeting, said a press release......Press Release Distribution

item.title: Janata Bank holds Cumilla divisional conference
item.description: The Branch Managers’ Conference of Janata Bank’s Cumilla divisional office was held on Wednesday......

item.title: Pubali Bank opens booth, hands over two buses to SUST
item.description: Pubali Bank PLC recently inaugurated the ‘SUST Electronic Banking Booth’ and handed over two student buses to Shahjalal University of Science and Technology, Sylhet to facilitate improved transportation services for students......

item.title: Islami Bank extends support for Palestinian students
item.description: Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC on Sunday signed a tripartite memorandum of understanding with the University of Dhaka and the Embassy of Palestine, aiming to provide educational stipends to Palestinian female students......

item.title: Singer launches World Cup for everyone campaign
item.description: Singer Bangladesh recently launched its ‘World Cup for Everyone’ campaign, aiming to bring the excitement, emotion, and immersive viewing experience of the FIFA World Cup closer to every household across Bangladesh......Bangladeshi Culture Course

item.title: Trust Bank, BB partners for two refinance schemes
item.description: Trust Bank PLC has signed two agreements on Monday for refinance schemes initiated by Bangladesh Bank to support the development of CMSME clusters and MSMEs across the country......

item.title: US accepts waiver of Iran oil sanctions
item.description: A source close to the Iranian negotiating team told Tasnim that, unlike its previous texts, the United States has accepted in its new proposal to waive Iran’s oil sanctions during the negotiation period....

item.title: Bank Asia holds training on remittance protection alongside agent banking business conference
item.description: Bank Asia Agent Banking organised a capacity-building training on ‘Remittance Surokkha’ alongside the ‘Agent Banking Business Conference–2026’ at the BRAC Learning Centre in Cumilla on Saturday......

item.title: Citizens Bank holds training on Islamic Banking operations
item.description: Citizens Bank recently organised a training program on “Islamic Banking Operations” at its training academy......Geographic Reference

item.title: MEASLES: HC order on alleged state negligence today
item.description: The High Court on Monday set today for passing an order on a writ petition seeking directive to form a body to probe alleged negligence of the authorities in handling the nationwide measles outbreak of 2026 that reportedly claimed the lives of 343 children....

item.title: Nagad offers cashbacks for Eid-ul-Azha shopping
item.description: Nagad is offering attractive cashbacks ahead of Eid-ul-Adha to ease festive shopping......

item.title: Dangerous legacy of reckless lending must stop
item.description: THE disclosure that five failed Islamic banks held collateral worth only a quarter of their outstanding loans once again exposes the alarming depth of malpractice that has for long plagued the banking sector. According to the Bangladesh Bank, the banks possessed collateral worth only Tk 45,000 crore against loans amounting to Tk 1,92,000 crore, while defaulted loans alone......

item.title: Unauthorised cattle market sign of governance failure
item.description: AS EID-UL-AZHA approaches, seasonal cattle traders are once again setting up markets with little regard for public inconvenience. The Dhaka city authorities oversee cattle market operations, and the lease process for 27 temporary cattle markets has already been completed, 16 under the Dhaka North City Corporation and 11 under the Dhaka South City Corporation......

item.title: Tracing BNP’s metamorphosis
item.description: ONE quote from the depths of antiquity resonates across time. It is attributed to the Greek philosopher Heraclitus of Ephesus, who was preoccupied with the changing nature of the universe. The quote in Greek is Panta Rhei, which roughly translates as ‘everything flows’. Change is the only constant. The cosmos is in an eternal state of flux. Two millennia later......

item.title: Water-based ecosystem as future development alternative
item.description: BANGLADESH, a riverine nation with an extensive network of rivers, haors, beels, baors, wetlands and chars, possesses a unique natural advantage. These water-based landscapes are not merely ecological assets; they also represent a vast and largely underutilised economic frontier. As climate change intensifies, cultivable land shrinks and rural unemployment continues to......

item.title: Higher education must move beyond expansion
item.description: HIGHER education has historically shaped the intellectual, scientific and economic foundations of societies. From ancient centres of learning such as Nalanda and Al Azhar to the modern research universities of Europe and North America, universities have continuously evolved in response to changing social and economic realities. In the twenty-first century, however, the......

item.title: $100 billion apparel dream needs a clear roadmap
item.description: BANGLADESH’S ready-made garment sector has long been celebrated as the backbone of the national economy. Over the past three decades, the industry has transformed the country into one of the world’s largest apparel exporters, generated millions of jobs, particularly for women, and positioned Bangladesh as a major player in global manufacturing. Against this......

item.title: Delaying Iran attack at Gulf leaders’ request: Trump
item.description: Trump’s sudden announcement on social media came after Iran said it had responded to a new US proposal aimed at ending the war......

item.title: Man killed, wife injured in Khulna burglary attack
item.description: A man was killed and his wife injured in an attack by burglars who broke into their house by cutting through the mud wall in Madhya Maheshwaripur area under Koyra in Khulna early Monday....

item.title: Proctor among 6 injured in IU clash
item.description: AT LEAST six people, including the proctor of Islamic University in Kushtia, were injured in a clash between two groups of students over a dispute on a campus bus that later escalated into violence near the university main gate on Sunday night....

item.title: Experts divided over project fate
item.description: Experts are divided over the fate of the country’s first bus rapid transit line connecting Gazipur to the Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport in the capital....

item.title: Gunmen in Pakistan kill two police officers
item.description: Gunmen in northwest Pakistan killed two police officers protecting polio vaccination workers on Monday, a security official said, on the first day of a nationwide campaign to counter rising cases....

item.title: CEC urges parties to reach consensus for peaceful local polls
item.description: Chief election commissioner AMM Nasir Uddin on Monday called on the political parties to sit together and reach consensus to ensure peaceful local government elections to be held soon....

item.title: Rehana’s call asking Salman to flee played at ICT
item.description: A phone conversation between deposed prime minister Sheikh Hasina’s sister Sheikh Rehana and former industries adviser Salman F Rahman, recorded on August 5, 2024 during the student-led mass uprising, was played before International Crimes Tribunal-1......

item.title: Lebanon president vows to stop war with Israel
item.description: Lebanese president Joseph Aoun said Monday that he would do the ‘impossible’ in order to stop the war with Israel, after a ceasefire and direct talks between the countries failed to end the fighting....

item.title: 30 wounded in Russian attacks on Ukraine overnight
item.description: Russia launched hundreds of drones and nearly two dozen missiles at Ukraine overnight wounding more than 30 people, Ukrainian officials said on Monday, as Kyiv and Moscow step up deadly long-range attacks....

item.title: Major rivers swell amid heavy rain
item.description: Major rivers continued to swell on Monday amid heavy rainfall in several divisions and upstream Indian regions, with the trend likely to persist over the next three to five days, according to the Flood Forecasting and Warning Centre....

item.title: Arakan Army kills 170 at Rakhine village in a day: HRW research
item.description: The Human Rights Watch, in a recent study, revealed that Myanmar’s rebel armed group, the Arakan Army, killed at least 170 Rohingya men, women and children — and likely injured or killed hundreds more — in Hoyyar Siri village, known in Burmese......

item.title: 377 deer poachers arrested in one year in the Sundarbans
item.description: Forest guards in the eastern part of the Sundarbans arrested 377 people in forest crime cases during drives conducted over the past one year and seized more than 1 lakh feet of deer trapping nets along with 249 kilograms of venison and 448 trawlers and boats....

item.title: Staff shortage cripples healthcare services at Sunamganj Sadar Hospital
item.description: The 250-bed Sunamganj Sadar Hospital has been grappling with a severe manpower shortage, crippling healthcare services at the district’s largest public medical facility and forcing patients to endure long waits, inadequate treatment and mounting frustration....

item.title: Qatari labour minister calls on PM
item.description: Qatari labour minister Ali bin Saeed bin Samikh Al Marri paid a courtesy call on prime minister Tarique Rahman discussed bilateral issues on Sunday evening....

item.title: BMU holds seminar on measles resurgence in Bangladesh
item.description: Bangladesh Medical University held a seminar titled Measles Resurgence in Bangladesh: Today’s Infection, Tomorrow’s Risk at the BMU in Dhaka on Monday....Bangladeshi Culture Course

item.title: GAU concludes e-GP, VAT management training
item.description: Gazipur Agricultural University concluded its five-day training on Bangladesh e-government procurement system, tax and VAT deduction at the seminar hall of GAU’s Institutional Quality Assurance Cell on Monday....

item.title: Deposits through agent banks top Tk 50,000cr
item.description: Bangladesh’s agent banking sector continued its steady expansion in the first quarter of 2026, with deposit and loan disbursement witnessing over 14 per cent growth......

item.title: International Museum Day observed
item.description: With the slogan ‘Museums Uniting a Divided World,’ the International Museum Day 2026 was observed on Monday with diverse events organised by the Bangladesh National Museum on its premises....

item.title: DU IR department holds orientation for new students
item.description: The international relations department of Dhaka University held an orientation for its new students at the department on Monday....Maps

item.title: Unrealistic budget may deepen economic pressure: Debapriya
item.description: Centre for Policy Dialogue distinguished fellow Debapriya Bhattacharya on Monday warned that Bangladesh’s upcoming budget must reflect economic realities, saying unrealistic revenue and spending targets could worsen economic pressure......

item.title: ‘The Portrait of an Adivasi Beloved’ launched
item.description: A collection of poems titled ‘The Portrait of an Adivasi Beloved’—a translation by Alamgir Mohammad based on Ehsan Mahmud’s poetry collection Adivasi Premikar Mukh—was launched at the Bangla Academy in Dhaka on Monday....

item.title: Tourism can be key driver of jobs: state minister
item.description: The State Minister for Civil Aviation and Tourism, M Rashiduzzaman Millat, has said tourism will play a key role in employment generation and strengthening the national economy......

item.title: Inclusion of marginalised people in budget process demanded
item.description: Researchers, rights activists, journalists and civil society members on Monday called for ensuring the participation of marginalised communities in the formulation and implementation of the national budget....

item.title: ‘Orange bond’ launched on DSE e-platform
item.description: Dhaka Stock Exchange and BRAC EPL Investments Limited today signed a landmark agreement to facilitate electronic subscription of the country’s first ‘Orange Bond’ through the capital market platform, marking a significant step in thematic and impact-based financing in Bangladesh......

item.title: Govt to cut bank loan to spur private credit
item.description: The government is set to implement a strategic shift in the national budget for fiscal year 2026-27 by significantly reducing its reliance on internal debt, particularly from the banking sector, according to an official working on budget preparation......

item.title: NBR asks customs to work in Eid
item.description: The National Board of Revenue (NBR) has directed all customs houses and stations across the country to remain operational on a limited scale during the upcoming Eid al-Azha holidays to ensure uninterrupted import and export activities......
